description: Embedded data reference canonicalization. The body contains a single data_refs entry with embedded JSON content. JCS sorts keys inside the data ref ('embedded' < 'type'), inside the embedded object ('content' < 'encoding'), and inside the embedded content ('score' alone).

vectors.name: Body with embedded JSON data ref
vectors.description: Producer-supplied body containing one embedded data ref. Demonstrates that embedded.content (which is itself JSON for encoding=json) is canonicalized as part of the parent body.
implementer_notes: Inside data_refs[0]: the original input order is type, embedded; canonical order is embedded, type (alphabetical).
Inside data_refs[0].embedded: original order is encoding, content; canonical order is content, encoding.
data_refs is an array; array order is preserved. Producers MUST establish a stable array order before signing.

Note: when encoding=json, the embedded content is canonicalized as part of the parent body. When encoding=base64 or utf8, the content is a string and is not re-canonicalized; the data_ref's own content_hash (if present) is computed over the decoded bytes per RFC-ACDP-0002 §6.3.
